notes: Grade the conversion DECISIONS the skill encodes that a no-skill baseline gets wrong: the trap is bare `uvx markitdown` (base package, fails on real office files) vs the required `--from 'markitdown[all]'` form; preflighting `uv`/`uvx` presence; sensible default output filename; routing scanned PDFs through the az-doc-intel extra; and that wrapping via uvx adds NO repo dependency. Each prompt is an INLINE Q&A that forbids plan-mode/tools — the harness runs executors read-only, so the discriminating signal is the inline answer. A naive answer tends to suggest bare `uvx markitdown` or `pip install markitdown`; the skill's guidance does not.

evals.name: all-extra-not-bare-uvx
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. I have a Word file at /tmp/contract.docx and I want it as Markdown using the markitdown tool via uvx. Give the exact one-line command, and explain what goes wrong if I just run `uvx markitdown /tmp/contract.docx`.
assertions: Uses `uvx --from 'markitdown[all]' markitdown` — the [all] extra form, with the spec quoted
Explains bare `uvx markitdown` installs the BASE package only (no docx/pdf/office extras) and fails or returns empty/garbled output on real office files
Writes the output with `-o` to a sensible path such as /tmp/contract.md (input stem + .md, same directory)
Does NOT tell the user to `pip install markitdown` or otherwise add a dependency

evals.name: preflight-uv-presence
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. Before I convert a PDF to Markdown with the uvx-based markitdown approach, what should be checked first, and what do I do if it's not there?
assertions: Preflights that `uv` / `uvx` is available (e.g. `command -v uv`) before attempting the conversion
If missing, points to installing uv via the astral.sh installer (curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh)
Notes uvx runs markitdown in an ephemeral/cached environment so nothing is installed into the repo (no dependency added)
Mentions the conversion command still uses the `--from 'markitdown[all]'` form, not bare `uvx markitdown`

evals.name: scanned-pdf-az-doc-intel
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. I ran markitdown on a scanned PDF (it's just page images, no embedded text) and got an empty Markdown file. Why, and how do I actually get the text out?
assertions: Explains the default local extraction can't read image-only / scanned PDFs (no embedded text → empty output)
Recommends the Azure Document Intelligence path — the `markitdown[all,az-doc-intel]` extra with `-d` and `-e $AZURE_DOC_INTEL_ENDPOINT`
Notes this is gated / opt-in (requires the endpoint env var set; a paid external service), not the default
Does NOT claim the standard `[all]` local path will OCR a scanned/image-only PDF

evals.name: no-repo-dependency-default-output
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. I want to add document-to-Markdown conversion to this repo, but it's a stdlib-only, no-dependency project. Can I use markitdown without violating that, and where does the converted file land by default?
assertions: Confirms it adds NO repo dependency — markitdown is run via `uvx` (ephemeral, cached env), nothing pip-installed or added to requirements/venv
States the conversion command is `uvx --from 'markitdown[all]' markitdown <input> -o <output.md>` (the [all] extra form)
Default output is the input's stem with a .md suffix next to the source (e.g. report.pdf → report.md); without -o it goes to stdout
Does NOT recommend `pip install markitdown` or adding it as a project dependency

evals.name: prerelease-pin-for-latest
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. I want the newest markitdown (0.1.6) for its PDF fixes, but `uvx --from 'markitdown[all]' markitdown --version` keeps giving me 0.1.5 with no error at all. Why does this happen, what exact command gets me 0.1.6, and should that become my default invocation?
assertions: Explains the silent backtrack: markitdown 0.1.6's [all] extra pulls a PRE-RELEASE dependency (azure-ai-contentunderstanding>=1.2.0b1, via the Content Understanding converter) and uv disallows pre-releases by default, so the resolver quietly falls back to 0.1.5

Preferred fix: pin 0.1.6 with the standard non-Azure-CU extras so NO pre-release override is needed — e.g. `uvx --from 'markitdown[pdf,docx,xlsx,xls,pptx,outlook,audio-transcription,youtube-transcription]==0.1.6' markitdown ...`

Mentions `--prerelease=allow` with the pinned `[all]==0.1.6` only for when the Azure Content Understanding path itself is wanted, kept scoped to that invocation and NOT made the default (it broadens pre-release acceptance to every transitive dependency)
